Fear and Greed is at
27
(Fear)
this morning. Bitcoin bounced sharply to
$63K
over the long US holiday weekend from a 21-month low below $58K,
driven largely by short liquidations as thin order books
amplified the move. Whether that recovery holds is now the
central question heading into the week.

Meanwhile the structural calendar kept moving. Ethereum just had
its biggest protocol announcement in years. A senator moved to
ban officials from launching meme coins. And whale behaviour
on-chain is flashing a signal worth watching.

Let's dive in.

Latest News
-----------

Bitcoin ETF Outflows Just Hit a Record Streak

US spot Bitcoin ETFs shed approximately $527 million last
week, marking eight consecutive weeks of net outflows, the
longest streak since the products launched.

The run has now pulled billions from crypto products even as BTC
briefly recovered over the holiday weekend.

What was the single strongest inflow day during the record
outflow streak?

Read More  
( https://link.coinmarketcap.com/f/a/0sREvoeZa39B6KqiTnwFjg~~/AAQRxRA~/WsuAutH_nFFmErDsNnd7b1X7D5ljNbuiX2GNnANJ_9rTQNE6OPNk4o8ToNoY_NFAtogJcsUPTlyyUksBlRP3Xb3iSE2Se3xH3fCGsejOj8u1gVONwy-jzrASKnc4EKucjgP2Ioq3VYxGOxe9bDsAlmpY_hnwJvS0cF4hITnkIEM~ )
   A Senator Wants to Ban Officials From Launching Meme Coins

New York Senator Kirsten Gillibrand proposed on July 4 that
members of Congress, the US president, and the pres

75527.subject: The Most Practical Guide to Claude Fable 5 📚
75527.body:
View this post on the web at https://linas.substack.com/p/claude-fable-5-guide

👋 Hey, Linas here! Every day, I break down 3 stories shaping the future of FinTech & Artificial Intelligence - plus the money movements and trends worth tracking. First time here? 395k+ FinTech and AI leaders get this daily. Join them:
You have until the end of the day tomorrow, July 7, 2026. Through that date, Claude Fable 5 [ https://substack.com/redirect/a7d74973-cda1-4b5a-97d1-262b951d5f99?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ] is included in Pro, Max, Team, and select Enterprise plans for up to 50% of weekly usage limits; after it, every Fable session draws paid usage credits on top of your subscription. Anthropic says it intends to restore Fable to plans once capacity allows, with no committed date.
So the cheap window to learn the most capable model the public has ever had access to closes tomorrow, and everyone still using Fable 5 like it’s ChatGPT is quietly falling behind.
Most importantly, raw capability is only half the story. The other half: the model is no longer the bottleneck. You are.
Thariq Shihipar, an engineer on Anthropic’s Claude Code team, published a field guide on July 3 called A Field Guide to Fable: Finding Your Unknowns that drew two million views in its first three days. His argument, in a phrase he keeps returning to: the map is not the territory. Fable 5’s output quality is bottlenecked by your ability to close the gap between what you asked for and what actually needs to happen.
Most people simply conclude they need better prompts.
They actually need a better workflow. Most importantly, the people winning with Fable 5 aren’t writing longer prompts; they’re running a specific loop of moves before, during, and after implementation that surfaces the assumptions the model can’t read your mind about.
In this guide, you’ll get:
The map-vs-territory mental model that explains why so many Fable 5 outputs miss what you actually meant.
The 8-technique playbook, drawn from how Anthropic’s own Claude Code team works, split across the before, during, and after phases of implementation.
Prompting mechanics for Fable 5: the 4-part prompt structure, the 5 rules that move quality, and the 3 habits to drop today.
The autonomy playbook for running Fable 5 for hours or days without it drifting off-course.
Operational realities, including the Opus 4.8 fallback, the exact pricing math, and the geopolitical availability risk, that will determine whether you deploy Fable 5 correctly or expensively.
The five bets to place this week to make the 2x per-token premium pay for itself.
A downloadable Claude Skill that installs the entire “finding your unknowns” workflow into your Claude environment.
The included-in-your-plan Fable 5 window closes tomorrow. Below is the playbook that will separate the operators from the tourists in the second half of 2026.
The mental model: map vs. territory...

Unsubscribe https://subs

75526.subject: ❌ Alibaba bans Claude for spying
75526.body:
----------

## [Alibaba bans Claude for spying](https://www.tomshardware.com/tech-industry/artificial-intelligence/alibaba-bans-anthropics-claude-code-after-an-alleged-hidden-china-detection-backdoor-is-uncovered-employees-told-to-switch-to-qoder-as-the-rift-between-the-firms-widens)

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/aaae91df-88f4-4bd5-b681-0b921e58cb2d/Beehiiv_01_-_Jul_6.png?t=1783331518)
Caption: 

The US-China AI fight grew a spyware chapter.

Alibaba barred its 124,000 employees from Claude Code starting July 10, labeled it "high-risk software" and told everyone to switch to Qoder, its own coding agent.

It started when a Reddit user took Claude Code apart on June 30 and found hidden code, live since April with nothing in the release notes, that checked whether your clock was set to Shanghai or Urumqi and scanned your network for Chinese AI-lab addresses.

When it found one, it tucked a marker into the data sent back to Anthropic by swapping the apostrophe in "Today's date" for a look-alike character no human would ever spot.

Anthropic's Thariq Shihipar confirmed it, calling it a March experiment against resellers and distillation. That's the same distillation it accused Alibaba of in June, when it said 25,000 fake accounts siphoned 28.8 million answers out of Claude.

So the loop closes. Alibaba allegedly copied Claude, Anthropic bugged its own tool to catch the copies, and a stranger on Reddit found the bug. Now Alibaba has its excuse to tear Claude out and sell Qoder instead.
